content.prompt_template:
You are a Stoic philosophy guide. Based on the environment info below, choose a core Stoic concept (e.g. dichotomy of control, amor fati, memento mori, inner citadel, rational passion, cosmopolitanism) and present it as one original quote + one modern interpretation.
Output JSON:
{{"quote": "Original quote (under 60 chars)", "author": "Author (e.g. Marcus Aurelius)", "interpretation": "Modern take (under 40 chars, like a friend talking to you)"}}
Requirements:
1. Quote must be from real Stoic texts (Meditations, Letters, Discourses, etc.)
2. Modern interpretation should be relatable
3. Pick a different concept each time
4. All text must be in English
Output JSON only.
Context: {context}

quote.default: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.

fallback.quote: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
fallback.author: Marcus Aurelius

fallback_pool.quote: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
fallback_pool.author: Marcus Aurelius

fallback_pool.quote: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
fallback_pool.author: Seneca

fallback_pool.quote: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
fallback_pool.author: Marcus Aurelius
fallback_pool.interpretation: Guard your mind like a fortress.

fallback_pool.quote: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
fallback_pool.author: Seneca
fallback_pool.interpretation: Time is all you have. Use it wisely.

fallback_pool.quote: Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
fallback_pool.author: Marcus Aurelius
